title: Today's AI Brief: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
summary: A look at Endava's AI-powered software delivery and Meta's AI-generated clickbait

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got two interesting stories to share with you, both from the world of artificial intelligence. First up, we're looking at how Endava is using AI agents to shake up software delivery.

Endava is teaming up with OpenAI to leverage AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex. This means they can accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build a culture that's native to AI across their entire organization. It's a big deal because it shows how AI can change the way we work, making things faster and more efficient.

Next, I came across a story about Meta creating its own AI-generated clickbait news feed. Apparently, it was a test that didn't quite go as planned. After some questions from The Verge, Meta decided to pull the feature. It's an interesting example of how AI can be used to create content, but also raises questions about the line between automation and manipulation.

I'm not sure about you, but I find it fascinating to see how AI is being tested and implemented in different ways. From software delivery to content creation, it's clear that AI is here to stay. My quick read is that we're just starting to scratch the surface of what's possible with AI, and it's going to be interesting to see where this technology takes us.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
